message:
feat(cli/models): wire semantic backfill scheduler into `cass models backfill`

First consumer of the scheduler vocabulary added in the previous
semantic.rs commit: `cass models backfill` gains a `--scheduled`
flag (alias `--background`) that gates the backfill run through the
`semantic_backfill_scheduler_decision` policy before spending any
CPU or thread budget.

- `ModelsCommand::Backfill` grows a `scheduled: bool` (visible_alias
  "background") between `batch_conversations` and `data_dir`. Default
  is `false` so existing scripted invocations keep their opt-in
  "run right now" semantics; `--scheduled` is the new
  "respect the governor" path for systemd timers and cron-style
  callers.
- Thread the flag through the `run_models_command` dispatch and down
  into `run_models_backfill(..., scheduled, ...)`.
- In `run_models_backfill`, when `scheduled` is true:
    * Resolve `SemanticPolicy` via `CliSemanticOverrides::default()`
      so the decision reflects the same policy the backfill loop
      would use.
    * Build `SemanticBackfillSchedulerSignals::from_env()` for the
      `CASS_SEMANTIC_BACKFILL_{FOREGROUND_ACTIVE,LEXICAL_REPAIR_ACTIVE,
      FORCE,DISABLE}` flags.
    * Ask for a decision, stash it in `scheduler_decision`.
  If `decision.should_run()` is false:
    * Determine the structured-output format: if the caller
      requested `--format sessions`, downgrade to compact (since we
      have nothing to stream). Otherwise honor robot_format_from_env.
    * Print a structured status payload with `state`, `reason`,
      `next_step` (the human-readable hint baked into the enum),
      the scheduled vs. requested batch sizes, the capacity numbers,
      and the pause reasons that fed the decision.
    * Return without running any backfill work.

When the decision is Running, the rest of the existing backfill
pipeline proceeds with the scheduler-adjusted
`scheduled_batch_conversations` available in the decision record
(hookup into the loop happens in the next slice; this commit only
surfaces the gate).

The `--scheduled` flag is visible in `cass models backfill --help`
and accepts `--background` as an alias so systemd unit files and
docs can use the more readable name.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
